evals.prompt: You are asked to change the Remotion agent's instructions or behavior. Before making any code or agent instruction edits, write agent-v eval prompts that define the Red step of Red/Green/Refactor TDD and a clear failing condition. Return only the eval prompts, expected assertions, and the test-first plan.
evals.expected_output: A set of agent-v evaluation cases and a TDD-first plan stating that no production code or instruction changes may begin until the evals exist.
expectations: Creates agent-v eval prompts before any Remotion agent or code changes.
Defines a specific failing condition for the initial Red step.
States that Remotion agent changes are blocked until the eval definitions exist.
Uses Red/Green/Refactor language and mentions agent-v as the harness.

evals.prompt: You are validating a Remotion agent workflow change. Identify and return the exact rules the agent must enforce to ensure agent-v evals are written first, before any edits are applied to Remotion code or agent instructions.
evals.expected_output: A concise set of enforcement rules for Remotion agent TDD that can be used as agent-v eval expectations.
expectations: Lists explicit rules requiring agent-v eval creation before code edits.
Includes a rule forbidding production changes until the eval suite exists.
Refers to the Remotion agent workflow specifically, not general SDLC guidance.
